concerns.text: Task T032 mandates using the Lincoln-Petersen Capture-Recapture estimator for precision/recall. This contradicts the Spec (FR-004, SC-001) and Plan which require measuring against an expert-validated ground truth baseline derived from PR comments. There is no task covering the standard TP/FP/FN calculation against the human baseline defined in FR-006.

Task T034 specifies a 'Fixed-effects regression' model. The Spec (FR-008) and Plan (Complexity Tracking) explicitly require 'Mixed-effects regression' (LMM) to handle project-level clustering. No task exists to implement the mixed-effects model required by FR-008.

Task T036 implements Bonferroni correction. The Plan (Section: Computational Feasibility) explicitly rejects Bonferroni as 'too conservative' and mandates a 'max-t permutation procedure' for FWER control. No task exists to implement the max-t permutation procedure required by FR-008 and the Plan.

Task T028 implements a ±5 line tolerance window for sensitivity analysis. The Spec (FR-005) and Plan restrict this tolerance to a fallback mechanism when AST is unavailable, not as a primary sensitivity parameter. The sensitivity analysis required by FR-012 (keyword heuristic threshold) is covered by T025, but T028 implements a non-compliant variation of the tolerance logic.

Task T039 implements a post-hoc power analysis check. The Spec (Assumptions) and Plan treat the 30–40 repository sample size as a fixed constraint with mixed-effects models as the mitigation. A post-hoc power check on a fixed, small N is not required by any FR/SC and may contradict the design assumption that the sample size is adequate for the chosen model.

Task T002a includes `sentence-transformers` in requirements. While the Plan allows `all-MiniLM-L6-v2` (CPU-capable), the task description does not explicitly verify CPU-only execution constraints or clarify the library's role, creating ambiguity against the Plan's strict 'No GPU' constraint.
